mapping.a_element: the full interacting Falicov-Kimball Hamiltonian being reduced to a tractable description
mapping.b_element: the full interacting matrix-model/QCD Hamiltonian being reduced to an effective operator

mapping.a_element: generalized-Gibbs-ensemble description of the reduced steady state
mapping.b_element: effective small Hamiltonian projected onto a reduced basis and diagonalized

brief_justification: The only overlap is the generic goal of reducing an interacting many-body Hamiltonian to a smaller effective description; the actual machinery differs. Paper A studies real-time non-equilibrium relaxation after an interaction quench (non-equilibrium DMFT time evolution, collapse-and-revival, a generalized Gibbs ensemble built from conserved quantities), whereas Paper B performs a perturbative similarity-renormalization-group unitary flow that band-diagonalizes a static Hamiltonian to extract a bound-state energy — no shared governing equation, threshold, or role-for-role dynamical correspondence.
